author: user_855de31d

text:
Please stop pushing forward all the “Made for Your Name” playlists. I WANT the feeling of clicking onto a stream of curated music that someone else, somewhere, might be hearing and experiencing the same as me! It’s so lonely to listen to music allegedly optimized for me, but which is actually isolating me from a feeling of connectedness to others. 
Also, the algorithm that’s making these lists is not good. Every SINGLE one has Girl Put Your Records On 🤡 I do not like this song! But maybe bc I don’t bother to skip it, it keeps getting more plays? Ugh I hate my rabbit hole! How did I get trapped here? I want to go hang out with the other rabbits! Does anyone want a list extrapolated from their first random hour on the app, endlessly reinforced?
title: So over the personalized playlists. :(
author: user_a66a5f40

text: There’s no way to report problems from the phone app and I rarely use it on the computer but there are glitches that should be reported when they happen. Foreign artists I’ve found during my travels don’t show up when searching their full artist names and that’s really weird, someone can send me the music through links but it should be enough to search them. There should be ways to hide podcasts (either that you tried and don’t like or something that you don’t want on your screen, for kids/work/etc). Plus every time there’s an app update or an Apple update the app acts up beyond need for use. I went from using it at least four hours per day to sometimes not opening it for two days simply because it doesn’t work. Was my favorite app for 8 years and 2026 sucks.
title: Improving

text: It’s decent all around, I’ve only have 2 things I don’t like. When you’re listening to a song off of your playlist and you want to shuffle the whole playlist to mix it up, when you hit play, it just plays the song already playing. I want it to shuffle it with a new song. Next, when you are listening to your playlist, it doesn’t allow you to view all of what you previously listened to unless you skip the songs back, sometimes it’s nice to know what was just playing.

I definitely recommend it to anyone who loves music.
